The '''Andalese''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of bipedal [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] known in the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] by [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]]. Largely resembling [[Human/Legends|Humans]] in appearance, their distinguishing features included small, pointed horns on the head and [[symbiosis graft]]s on the body. [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] suspected [[Enara]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Fallanassi/Legends|Fallanassi]] adept active during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], to be an Andalese.

==Biology and appearance==
The Andalese were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that had a basic [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] appearance, with two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and two legs that provided an upright, bipedal posture. They grew [[Hair/Legends|hair]] on their heads, on which they also had small, pointed horns that could be concealed by the hair, outward facing ears kin to that of other mammalian creatures as well as both males and females sporting long hairless prehensile tails. Another Andalese physical trait was the application of [[symbiosis graft]]s to their bodies. As they otherwise resembled [[Human/Legends|Humans]], the Andalese could pass for members of that species if they dressed in reasonably loose [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] and hid their horns. Nevertheless, they were distinct enough that, even when not flaunting their identity, they could be recognized by other features or mannerisms. The species has two [[Gender/Legends|sexes]], male and female.

==Society and culture==
At least some Andalese had access to [[Spacer/Legends|spacefaring]] technology by the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Some members of the species were known to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]].<ref name="Basic" />

==History==
The Andalese were known to the greater [[galactic community]] by [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]].<ref name="17 ABY">Both ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' place the events of ''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]'' trilogy to [[16 ABY/Legends|16]]–[[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]]. Since ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'', in which the Andalese are mentioned, is the last book of the trilogy, this article assumes that the events depicted in it take place in 17 ABY.</ref> That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], the Human New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was held captive aboard the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Intimidator (Executor-class)|''Pride of Yevetha'']] during the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], a confrontation between the [[Yevetha]]n [[Duskhan League|government]] and the New Republic. Among other [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] onboard was [[Enara]]—a member of<ref name="Tyrant's Test" /> the all-female<ref name="EGTF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> [[Fallanassi/Legends|Fallanassi]] order<ref name="Tyrant's Test" /> devoted to an aspect of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] that they called the [[White Current/Legends|White Current]]<ref name="EGTF" />—whom Solo believed to be possibly Andalese, despite her appearance indicating she was a baseline Human.<ref name="Tyrant's Test" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Andalese species was mentioned in ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'', the [[1997]] third book of ''[[The Black Fleet Crisis]]'' trilogy by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]]. Although indicating that an Andalese could pass for a Human, the novel does not establish whether they are a [[near-Human]] species<ref name="Tyrant's Test" /> that shares common biological ancestry with baseline Humans<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> or whether they have developed similar physical characteristics independently.<ref name="Tyrant's Test" />
